content.full_text:
RAVE MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
December 20, 1996
Dorm Hall
Councilmember
City of Imperial Beach
825 Imperial Beach Boulevard
Imperial Beach, California 91932
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-322
Dear Mr. Hall:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions ofthe Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
May you participate in a governmental decision regarding property that is within 300 feet
of property owned by a source of income to you?
CONCLUSION
No. You may not make, participate in malcing, or influence any governmental decision
involving the property within 300 feet of your sourceof income’s property unless the decision
will have no financial effect on your source of income’s property.
FACTS
You are a city councilmember in the City of Imperial Beach and you are also a licensed
architect. The city is considering the design approval of a multi-million dollar development
project.
The port district is designing the project, but the project will go through the city
Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18000 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Our File No. A-96-322
Page 2
planning process and the city council must approve the project’s design. Ifthe city approves the
design, then the project will go forward. If the city does not approve the design, the project
architects will be required to make modifications and then resubmit the design to the city. The
overall project has already been approved. Therefore, ifthe city rejects the design, it will delay,
but not end, the project. You received more than $250 in income from a property owner whose
property is within 300 feet of the proposed development.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. As a member ofthe Imperial Beach City Council, you are a
public official as defined in Section 82048.
Section 87103 specifies that an official has a financial interest within the meaning of
Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the official or any member of
his or her immediate family or on any source of income to the official of more than $250
received by or promised to the official in the past 12 months. (Section 87 103(c).) The
Commission has adopted several regulations to determine when a financial interest is affected by
a decision in a material way. (Regulations 18702-18702.6.)
If the source of income is an individual and is indirectly involved in the decision, the
effect of a decision is material if:
(a) The decision will affect the individual’s income, investments,
or other tangible or intangible assets or liabilities (other than real
property) by $1,000 or more; or
(b) The decision will affect the individual’s real property interest in
a manner that is considered material under Section 18702.3 or
Section 18702.4.
(Regulation 18702.6.)
In this case, your source of income is an individual with an ownership interest in real
property within 300 feet of the property that is the subject ofthe decision. The effect of a
decision is material as to real property ifthe property “is located within a 300 foot radius of the
boundaries (or the proposed boundaries) ofthe property which is the subject of the decision,
unless the decision will have no fmancial effect” on the real property interest. (Regulation
1 8702.3(a)(l).)


Our File No. A-96-322
Page 3
Therefore, the decision would have to have no financial effect on your source of income’s
real property interest. The project area is a full-scale redevelopment ofthe Pier Plaza area, with
landscaping, additional pedestrian area, and upgraded restroom facilities. Your source of income
has property within 300 feet ofthis redevelopment area. It appears reasonably foreseeable that
such a large scale improvement so close to your source of income’s property will have some
fmancial effect on that property. As a result, you may not participate in any decision regarding
the development absent an exception.
There is an exception to the conflict of interest rules. A public official is not required to
disqualify from a decision that will have a reasonably foreseeable material fmancial effect on the
official’s economic interest ifthat effect is indistinguishable from the effect on the public
generally. (Section 87103.) Regulation 18703 provides that an official may participate if the
governmental decision will affect a significant segment ofthe population in substantially the
same manner as the decision will affect the official’s economic interests. We do not have enough
facts to indicate whether the public generally exception will apply in your case, however, we
have enclosed a copy of Regulation 18703. If you need assistance in determining whether the
exception will apply, please contact our office at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By: Liane Randolph
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:LR:alc

QUESTION: May you participate in a governmental decision regarding property that is within 300 feet
of property owned by a source of income to you?

CONCLUSION: No. You may not make, participate in malcing, or influence any governmental decision
involving the property within 300 feet of your sourceof income’s property unless the decision
will have no financial effect on your source of income’s property.
